text.title: Django Coding Practices Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the practices-django-coding methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Business logic and validation MUST live on models (clean() + methods) or services. Views MUST be thin: parse input -> call model/service -> render response. View methods MUST NOT exceed 30 lines.
rule.rationale: Fat views are untestable without HTTP; logic on the model is unit-testable in isolation and reusable from management commands, signals, and tasks.

rule.statement: Every FK access inside a loop MUST be preceded by .select_related(...) for one-to-one/FK and .prefetch_related(...) for M2M/reverse relations. N+1 queries are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without eager loading, each iteration hits the DB; performance collapses under load. select_related does JOINs; prefetch_related does a second batched query.

rule.statement: Any view, service, or task that performs multiple related writes MUST wrap them in transaction.atomic (decorator or context manager). Partial-write states are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without atomicity, a failure halfway through leaves inconsistent state. Atomic blocks roll back to a savepoint on exception.

rule.statement: Domain queries MUST live on a custom QuerySet exposed via .as_manager(). Repeated .filter() chains in views are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Custom QuerySets make queries discoverable, named, and testable; spread-out filters duplicate logic and drift apart.

rule.statement: Signals (post_save, pre_delete) MUST be used only for cross-app concerns. In-app side-effects MUST live in model save() overrides or service functions.
rule.rationale: Signals run implicitly; agents lose track of side-effects. Explicit calls are easier to reason about and test.

rule.statement: settings.py MUST be split into base.py + dev.py + prod.py (or env-aware loaders). Secrets MUST come from env vars (django-environ or os.environ), never committed.
rule.rationale: Single-file settings get patched per environment and drift; split settings make per-env diffs reviewable.
